channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: South Africa rolls out game-changing HIV shot amid funding shortfalls
item.description: A new twice-yearly HIV prevention injection could transform South Africa's fight against the epidemic — but U.S. aid cuts and limited doses threaten to slow its impact.
item.content:encoded:
A new twice-yearly HIV prevention injection could transform South Africa's fight against the epidemic — but U.S. aid cuts and limited doses threaten to slow its impact.

(Image credit: Ihsaan Haffe)
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: The U.S. adds 172,000 jobs as the labor market picks up steam
item.description: U.S. employers added jobs for the third month in a row in May, while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%. But wage gains softened and likely failed to keep pace with rising prices.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. employers added jobs for the third month in a row in May, while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%. But wage gains softened and likely failed to keep pace with rising prices.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: China's highest bridge brings tourists and internet access to remote communities
item.description: A link to the highway that cuts travel times from hours to just minutes, and a symbol of a flow of investment that has provided unprecedented access to high-speed internet in this remote region.
item.content:encoded:
A link to the highway that cuts travel times from hours to just minutes, and a symbol of a flow of investment that has provided unprecedented access to high-speed internet in this remote region.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Senate passes immigration bill. And, Jill Biden discusses her husband's 2024 campaign
item.description: The Senate passed a $70 billion immigration enforcement funding bill after an overnight vote. And, former first lady Jill Biden discusses her husband's 2024 campaign with NPR's Newsmakers.
item.content:encoded:
The Senate passed a $70 billion immigration enforcement funding bill after an overnight vote. And, former first lady Jill Biden discusses her husband's 2024 campaign with NPR's Newsmakers.

(Image credit: Kent Nishimura)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The World Cup vs. bugs, germs and heat: Here's the game plan
item.description: Despite a diminished federal presence, public health departments are preparing for common ailments that could afflict fans who gather for the event — and are keeping an eye on the Ebola outbreak, too.
item.content:encoded:
Despite a diminished federal presence, public health departments are preparing for common ailments that could afflict fans who gather for the event — and are keeping an eye on the Ebola outbreak, too.

(Image credit: Todd Kirkland/FIFA)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Senate Republicans pass immigration funding after overnight vote
item.description: After a marathon 18-hour vote, the Senate has funded immigration enforcement. The GOP bill funds ICE and the Border Patrol for three years.
item.content:encoded:
After a marathon 18-hour vote, the Senate has funded immigration enforcement. The GOP bill funds ICE and the Border Patrol for three years.

(Image credit: Kent Nishimura)
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: Senate passes $70B immigration enforcement bill without limits on Trump settlement fund
item.description: The Senate passed legislation to fund President Donald Trump's immigration enforcement agencies early Friday morning, after weeks of delays and fierce backlash to an unrelated $1.776 billion settlement fund that threatened to derail the bill.
item.content:encoded:
The Senate passed legislation to fund President Donald Trump's immigration enforcement agencies early Friday morning, after weeks of delays and fierce backlash to an unrelated $1.776 billion settlement fund that threatened to derail the bill.

(Image credit: J. Scott Applewhite)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The quiz tracked Trump's wins and losses this week. Can you win bigly?
item.description: Plus, Serena Williams, Peabo Bryson, Kalshi and United Airlines make an appearance. Have you been paying attention?
item.content:encoded: Plus, Serena Williams, Peabo Bryson, Kalshi and United Airlines make an appearance. Have you been paying attention?
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: My husband planned our 'just us' trip and it was perfect. Here's why it worked
item.description: For a smooth, rewarding and drama-free couples trip, take as much stress out of the equation as you can — and get comfortable with the fact that a fight may arise (and that's OK).
item.content:encoded: For a smooth, rewarding and drama-free couples trip, take as much stress out of the equation as you can — and get comfortable with the fact that a fight may arise (and that's OK).
item.dc:creator: Amelia Edelman

item.title: The Supreme Court has left limited alternatives for protecting minority voting rights
item.description: After a major Supreme Court ruling, state-level voting rights acts and redistricting strategies in Democratic-led states are among the limited ways left for protecting racial-minority voters' power.
item.content:encoded:
After a major Supreme Court ruling, state-level voting rights acts and redistricting strategies in Democratic-led states are among the limited ways left for protecting racial-minority voters' power.

(Image credit: Mike Stewart)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang
